claim: No production deployment has occurred; this is a deployment-readiness analysis. No production metrics are reported.

lessons.statement: The action layer is the binding safety constraint: a correct diagnosis does not license a safe action; trap-actions must be penalized/blocked independently.
The deterministic reward is a gameable proxy; a hedge diagnosis plus a legitimate fix scores 0.55, training non-committal behavior that is an action-safety risk in production.

Substrate fidelity bounds and biases everything learned: 54/61 canonical fixes resolve in the sim engine, but 7 are broken (4 wrong-target/wrong-tool, 3 unmodeled-metric KeyErrors) and hysteresis is unmodeled.
We lack clean outcome ground truth: of the real incidents, 18 have a sourced MTTR and 12 are unknown, so no MTTR improvement can be claimed.

gaps.name: Trap-action safety under distribution shift
gaps.why_open: The trap-action check matches a closed, author-enumerated (tool,target) vocabulary; it cannot catch traps no author listed, and production incidents are out-of-distribution by definition. No off-distribution trap-avoidance evidence exists.
gaps.validation_protocol: Build a held-out incident set whose root causes and trap actions are disjoint from the training vocabulary (closest available proxy: held-out CIDG slice; a true test needs a real/anonymized incident stream we do not yet have). Measure trap-proposal recall and separate agent-avoidance from safety-harness BLOCKs.
gaps.acceptance_gate: TARGET — NOT YET MEASURED: trap-proposal rate on held-out, vocabulary-disjoint incidents <= 1%, with the safety harness blocking >= 99% of any that slip through, over >= 100 incidents.

gaps.name: Shadow-mode results
gaps.why_open: The agent has never run in shadow mode (propose-only, human executes); there is zero shadow-mode data and no rollback path implemented in the loop, so any non-shadow first step is unacceptable.
gaps.validation_protocol: Run the agent read-only alongside on-call: same signals in, proposed diagnosis+remediation out, human executes, diff per incident. Capture action-agreement with on-call, trap-proposal events, time-to-proposed-diagnosis, and false-confident commits.
gaps.acceptance_gate: TARGET — NOT YET MEASURED: over >= 50 real incidents in shadow mode, action-agreement with on-call >= 80%, trap-proposal rate <= 1%, zero unrecoverable proposals, before any write access is granted.

gaps.name: MTTR
gaps.why_open: 12 of the real incidents have unknown MTTR and source MTTRs are unverified, so there is no clean baseline; MTTR also cannot be measured before shadow mode (G2) exists. No MTTR delta is claimed.
gaps.validation_protocol: Precondition: complete/verify the A9 MTTR labels (resolve the 12 unknowns or exclude with reason). Then in shadow mode measure time-to-correct-proposed-diagnosis vs human baseline; only after write access, measure true end-to-end MTTR against the labeled baseline on matched incident classes.
gaps.acceptance_gate: TARGET — NOT YET MEASURED: on verified-MTTR incidents only, agent-in-loop MTTR is statistically non-inferior to the human baseline before any improvement claim is made.

readiness_checklist.item: Deterministic reward not gameable by hedge/negation (D13 mitigations shipped)
Trap-safety validated on vocabulary-disjoint off-distribution incidents (G1)
Shadow-mode run with measured action-agreement and trap rate (G2)
Rollback / blast-radius limit implemented and tested in the loop
MTTR baseline labels completed and verified (A9 12 unknowns)
Scenario substrate authoring bugs fixed (A16: 7 broken fixes)
Engine models all SLO metrics the target cluster tracks (p99, pod_restarts)
Sim-engine validation of canonical fixes (A16: 54/61 resolve)
